steps.title: Track the repeated focus
steps.content: Every sentence develops Louis Graystone's effort to collect petition signatures. The comparison to tending a garden shows care and persistence. He keeps the petition with him at all times, notices tribal members who have not signed, and approaches them in town, on the road, outside public places, and during ordinary personal moments. The passage emphasizes his determination more than the community's changing views.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice A says attitudes toward the petition change over time, but the excerpt does not show a sequence of changing opinions. Choice B says most tribal members are enthusiastic, yet the passage stresses Louis's pursuit of signatures rather than people's eagerness. Choice D suggests refusal because of dislike for Louis, which the passage never states. The main purpose is to portray Louis Graystone's strong commitment to collecting signatures for the petition. The answer is C.
